title: Search shows wrong results — finding archived tasks instead of active ones
body: Something is wrong with search. When I search for tasks, I'm getting results that include old archived tasks that should be hidden, and I'm not seeing some active tasks that I know exist. For example, I searched for 'Q2 planning' and got an archived task from last year but not the active one I created yesterday. This is happening for all my searches. My teammate says she sees the same thing.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — that's definitely not the behavior you should be seeing.

To help us narrow this down: when you searched for 'Q2 planning,' where exactly were you searching? For example, were you using the main search bar, a filtered view within a project, or the API? And do you have any filters active (like status, date range, or assignee) when the wrong results appear?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I'm using the main search bar at the top of the page. The default filter is set to 'Active tasks only,' which is what I always use. I haven't changed any other filters — no specific date range, assignee, or status filters. The weird thing is that the results I'm getting look like they're all archived tasks, not active ones. I have about 150 active tasks and maybe 300 archived ones, and the search results seem to be pulling from the archived pile instead of the active one. If I switch the filter to 'Archived tasks,' I actually see my active tasks there, which is the opposite of what should happen.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's a really helpful detail — the fact that switching to 'Archived tasks' shows your active tasks tells us a lot. It sounds like the filter labels are effectively swapped.

One more thing that would help the team track this down quickly: do you remember roughly when this started? Was search working correctly last week, or did you notice it break after a specific point — maybe after a recent update or after some bulk operation on your tasks?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yes, it started about three days ago. Before that, search was working perfectly fine. I believe there was a system update around that time — we were moved to version 2.3.1. I didn't do any bulk operations on my tasks or change any settings. It just started happening after that update. My teammate noticed it around the same time too.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Search filter logic inverted since v2.3.1 — 'Active tasks only' returns archived tasks and vice versa","problem":"The main search bar's task-status filter is returning the opposite of what the label indicates. Selecting 'Active tasks only' surfaces archived tasks, while selecting 'Archived tasks' shows active tasks. This affects all search queries, not just specific terms.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The v2.3.1 update likely introduced a logic inversion in the search filter predicate — either a boolean flag was flipped, filter enum values were swapped, or a query condition was negated. The filter UI labels are correct, but the underlying query they map to is reversed.","reproduction_steps":["Log into TaskFlow on version 2.3.1","Navigate to the main search bar at the top of the page","Ensure the filter is set to 'Active tasks only' (the default)","Search for a term that matches both an active task and an archived task (e.g., 'Q2 planning')","Observe that archived tasks appear in results while known active tasks are missing","Switch the filter to 'Archived tasks'","Observe that active tasks now appear in the results"],"environment":"TaskFlow v2.3.1, web interface, main search bar. Confirmed across at least two users.","severity":"high","impact":"All users relying on the default search experience see incorrect results. Active tasks are effectively hidden and archived tasks are surfaced, degrading core search usability for every search query. Workaround exists (manually select the opposite filter), but users must know about it.","recommended_fix":"Diff the search filter logic between v2.3.0 and v2.3.1. Look for an inverted boolean, swapped enum mapping, or negated condition in the query that translates the 'Active'/'Archived' filter selection into a database or index query. The fix is likely a one-line inversion.","proposed_test_case":"Create one active task and one archived task with the same keyword. Search with 'Active tasks only' filter and assert only the active task is returned. Search with 'Archived tasks' filter and assert only the archived task is returned. Run this test against both v2.3.0 (should pass) and v2.3.1 (should fail pre-fix, pass post-fix).","information_gaps":["Whether the issue affects API-based search or only the web UI","Whether the inversion applies to other filter types (e.g., status, assignee) or only the active/archived toggle","Exact changelog or commit diff for v2.3.1 that touched search or filter logic"]}
